mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) sustained near zero reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) falling below the 9.0% threshold signals labor market stabilization and improved consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in Industrial Production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) confirms a broad-based supply-side recovery, reducing recession fears and boosting equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal (both series rising) fails to outperform the baseline market return by at least 50bps over 20 days, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in Housing Starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for the credit cycle and financial sector health, predicting positive S&P 500 returns during the post-crisis recovery phase.
failure_condition: Housing Starts increases are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in > 60% of cases, or the correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation (CPIAUCSL) preserves real returns and prevents central bank tightening, creating a favorable environment for risk assets and positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Periods with CPIAUCSL growth < 2.0% exhibit lower 20-day forward returns than periods with higher inflation, or the Rank IC for CPIAUCSL is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) turning positive (net job creation) confirms the end of the recession, driving consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are negative in > 50% of months where PAYEMS shows net job creation, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where Retail Sales (RSAFS) rise while Industrial Production (INDPRO) lags signals inventory drawdown and potential future production ramp-up, predicting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal (RSAFS up, INDPRO down) is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Large upward revisions in GDP (GDPC1) vintage data indicate stronger underlying demand than initially reported, boosting market sentiment and short-term equity returns in high-uncertainty regimes.
failure_condition: GDP revisions show no significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, or positive revisions are followed by negative returns in > 55% of instances.
